title: dispermotheca viscosa var. alpestris
intro: Dispermotheca viscosa var. alpestris, also known as the sticky alpine plant, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the bignoniaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dispermotheca viscosa var. alpestris include wound healing, skin soothing, and protection against infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and are often used topically to aid in skin care and wound treatment.
